MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-08-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER COUPONS. COM INCORPORATED'S (AMENDED) PETITION TO CORRECT AND CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED. THE ARBITRATOR COULD NOT HAVE BEEN CLEARER THAT HE WAS ISSUING AN INTERIM AWARD AND NOT A PARTIAL FINAL AWARD, THAT THERE WERE SEVERAL ISSUES YET TO HEAR EVIDENCE ON AND DECIDE, AND THAT THE JAMS RULES ALLOWED HIM TO PROCEED IN THAT MANNER. PURSUANT TO JAMS RULE 22(I), IF AN ARBITRATOR RE-OPENS A HEARING, THE TIME LIMITS SET FORTH BY THE JAMS RULES ARE EXTENDED UNTIL THE ARBITRATOR DECLARES THE HEARING CLOSED. HIS INTERPRETATION OF THE APPLICABLE JAMS RULES IS FINAL. THEREFORE, THERE IS NOTHING RIPE FOR COURT CONFIRMATION, AND THERE IS NO BASIS FOR THE COURT TO ALTER THE ARBITRATOR'S INTERIM AWARD. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT CATALINA MARKETING CORPORATION'S [REDACTED] MOTION TO FILE UNDER SEAL: 1) RESPONSE TO COUPONS.COM, INC'S PETITION TO CORRECT AND CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD 2) MEMORANDUM OF POINTS & AUTHORITIES 3) DECLARATION OF JAMES S. TSUEI IS GRANTED, BASED ON THE FINDINGS OF THE ARBITRATOR. PURSUANT TO CRC 2.550, THE COURT FINDS THAT (1) THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE RECORD; (2) THE OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THE RECORD; (3) A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE RECORD IS NOT SEALED; (4) THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED; AND (5) NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

(Amended) Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Correct And Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Oct-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: KELLY WOODRUFF, ESQ., 415.954.4400, FOR PETITIONER COUPONS.COM INCORPORATED.
EVETTE PENNYPACKER, ESQ. AND CLAUDE STERN, ESQ., 650.801.5000, FOR RESPONDENT CATALINA MARKETING CORPORATION.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PETITIONER COUPONS. COM INCORPORATED's (Amended) Petition To Correct And Confirm Arbitration Award is denied. The Arbitrator could not have been clearer that he was issuing an interim award and not a partial final award, that there were several issues yet to hear evidence on and decide, and that the JAMS Rules allowed him to proceed in that manner. Pursuant to JAMS Rule 22(i), if an Arbitrator re-opens a hearing, the time limits set forth by the JAMS Rules are extended until the Arbitrator declares the hearing closed. His interpretation of the applicable JAMS Rules is final. Therefore, there is nothing ripe for court confirmation, and there is no basis for the Court to alter the Arbitrator's interim award. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

[Redacted] Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To File Under Seal: 1) Response To Coupons.Com, Inc'S Petition To Correct And Confirm Arbitration Award 2) Memo Of Pts & Auth 3) Dec Of James S. Tsuei
Off Calendar Oct-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: KELLY WOODRUFF, ESQ., 415.954.4400, FOR PETITIONER COUPONS.COM INCORPORATED.
EVETTE PENNYPACKER, ESQ. AND CLAUDE STERN, ESQ., 650.801.5000, FOR RESPONDENT CATALINA MARKETING CORPORATION.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT CATALINA MARKETING CORPORATION's [Redacted] Motion To File Under Seal: 1) Response To Coupons.Com, Inc's Petition To Correct And Confirm Arbitration Award 2) Memorandum Of Points & Authorities 3) Declaration Of James S. Tsuei is granted, based on the findings of the Arbitrator. Pursuant to CRC 2.550, the Court finds that (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
